onboarding.subtitle: Get started in 3 simple steps to track your portfolio performance.
onboarding.step1_desc: Add your ETFs, stocks, bonds with their tickers in the config file or via the transaction form.
onboarding.step2_desc: Record your buys, sells, dividends and coupons. You can add them one by one or import a CSV.
onboarding.step3_desc: See returns, allocation, performance charts, risk metrics and rebalancing suggestions.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.subtitle: How every metric is calculated — formulas, assumptions, and data sources
methodology.source_borsa: Bond prices: Borsa Italiana (web scraping fallback)
methodology.source_ecb: Risk-free rate: ECB Deposit Facility Rate (data-api.ecb.europa.eu)
methodology.m_simple_return_short: Percentage gain relative to cost basis. Does not account for time.
methodology.m_simple_return_detail:
Measures how much you gained or lost as a percentage of what you invested.

Vmarket = current market value
Cbasis = total cost basis (sum of purchases minus cost of shares sold)

This is a point-in-time ROI. It does not account for how long you held the investment or when you added money.
methodology.m_twr_short: Performance of the strategy, independent of cash flow timing.
methodology.m_twr_detail:
Time-Weighted Return eliminates the impact of deposits and withdrawals. It measures how well the investments performed regardless of when you added money.

The period is split into sub-periods at each cash flow. Each sub-period return is calculated, then they are chain-linked (multiplied together).

Vbefore,i = portfolio value just before transaction i (previous holdings at today's prices)
Vafter,i-1 = portfolio value just after the previous transaction

TWR is the standard for comparing fund managers and benchmarks (GIPS compliant).
methodology.m_xirr_short: Your actual annualized return considering timing of all cash flows.
methodology.m_xirr_detail:
XIRR (Extended Internal Rate of Return) finds the annualized discount rate that makes the Net Present Value of all cash flows equal to zero.

CFi = cash flow at time i (negative for buys, positive for sells/dividends)
r = the rate we solve for
ti = years from first cash flow to CFi

This is the same as MWRR (Money-Weighted Rate of Return). It reflects your personal return including the timing of your investments.

Solved numerically using Brent's method (scipy.optimize.brentq).
methodology.m_xirr_example:
Invest 5,000€ on Jan 1, invest 5,000€ on Jul 1, portfolio worth 11,200€ on Dec 31.
XIRR ≈ +15.8% p.a.
methodology.m_volatility_short: Annualized standard deviation of daily returns. Measures variability.
methodology.m_volatility_detail:
Calculated from daily TWR returns, then annualized by multiplying by √252 (trading days per year).

ri = daily TWR return
r̄ = mean daily return
n = number of observations

Uses sample standard deviation (n-1 denominator). Higher volatility means more unpredictable returns.
methodology.m_sharpe_short: Excess return per unit of total risk. Risk-free rate from ECB.
methodology.m_sharpe_detail:
Measures how much extra return you earn for each unit of risk taken, compared to a risk-free investment.

Rp = annualized portfolio return (from TWR)
Rf = risk-free rate (ECB Deposit Facility Rate, currently ~2%)
σp = annualized volatility

Above 1.0 is considered good. Above 2.0 is excellent. Below 0 means you're underperforming the risk-free rate.
methodology.m_sortino_short: Like Sharpe but only penalizes downside volatility.
methodology.m_sortino_detail:
Uses downside deviation instead of total standard deviation. Only negative returns contribute to the risk measure.

σdown = √(Σ min(ri, 0)² / n) × √252

This is fairer for strategies with asymmetric returns (big gains, small losses). A high Sortino with a lower Sharpe means your volatility comes mostly from upside moves.
methodology.m_drawdown_short: Worst peak-to-trough decline, measured on TWR.
methodology.m_drawdown_detail:
At each point in time, drawdown measures how far the TWR has fallen from its previous highest value.

Using TWR (not portfolio value) ensures that new deposits don't create artificial peaks. Only market performance creates new highs.

Max Drawdown is the single worst value in the drawdown series — the deepest hole you would have experienced.
methodology.m_est_tax_short: Capital gains tax on unrealized profits at configured rate.
methodology.m_est_tax_detail:
Applied only to positive unrealized gains. If the position is at a loss, estimated tax is zero.

rate = capital_gains_rate from config (default 26% for Italy, 12.5% for government bonds)

This is an estimate — actual tax depends on regime (amministrato/dichiarativo), offsetting with losses, and holding period.
methodology.m_est_tax_example:
Market value: 12,000€, Cost basis: 10,000€, Rate: 26%
Tax = max(0, 12,000 - 10,000) × 0.26 = 520€
methodology.m_cost_basis_short: Average cost method. Adjusted on buys, reduced proportionally on sells.
methodology.m_cost_basis_detail:
Uses the weighted average cost method:

• On buy: cost basis increases by the net transaction value (minus accrued interest for bonds)
• On sell: cost basis decreases by (avg cost per share × shares sold)

The average cost per share is recalculated after each buy. Sells reduce the total cost proportionally but don't change the per-share average.

For bonds, accrued interest paid at purchase is excluded from cost basis (it's recovered at the next coupon).

tooltips.cost_basis: Total capital deployed across all positions, net of accrued interest for bonds.
tooltips.market_value: Current mark-to-market value of all holdings.
tooltips.total_pnl: Unrealized P&L (open positions) + realized P&L (closed positions). Income excluded.
tooltips.income: Cumulative dividends and bond coupons received since inception.
tooltips.xirr: Annualized money-weighted return (IRR). Reflects your actual return considering cash flow timing.
tooltips.avg_cost: Volume-weighted average purchase price. Adjusts with each new buy, unchanged on sells.
tooltips.weight: Position weight as percentage of total portfolio market value.
tooltips.pnl: Combined unrealized + realized profit or loss for this position.
tooltips.est_tax: Estimated capital gains tax on unrealized profits at configured rate. Zero on losses.
tooltips.income_col: Cumulative dividends and coupons received from this instrument.
tooltips.market_gain: Portfolio value change from market movements only. Excludes net new contributions.
tooltips.twr: Time-Weighted Return. Eliminates cash flow impact — measures pure investment performance.
tooltips.mwrr: Money-Weighted Return (IRR) for the period. Reflects actual investor experience including timing.
tooltips.volatility: Annualized standard deviation of daily TWR returns. Higher = more variable performance.
tooltips.sharpe_ratio: Excess return per unit of total risk. (Return - Risk Free) / Volatility. Above 1 is good.
tooltips.sortino_ratio: Excess return per unit of downside risk only. Ignores upside volatility. Higher is better.
tooltips.max_drawdown: Largest peak-to-trough decline in TWR. Measures worst-case loss from highest point.
